short: An open-source engine for serving LLMs fast by packing many requests onto a GPU.
definition: vLLM is a high-throughput inference and serving engine for large language models. Its headline innovation is PagedAttention, which manages the KV cache in small non-contiguous blocks the way an operating system pages RAM, eliminating memory fragmentation. Combined with continuous (iteration-level) batching, it keeps the GPU saturated and packs far more concurrent requests onto the same hardware than naive serving. It exposes an OpenAI-compatible API, making it a near drop-in replacement for hosted inference.
